slide_1_Cover: Multi-Type Fabric QC AI Software + Hardware Solution
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: Per-roll cost drops from $2.50 to $1.00 (60% reduction) while achieving 100% inspection coverage — eliminating sampling gaps entirely
7x detection accuracy with 10-15% monthly efficiency gain from combined cost reduction and defect capture uplift across production lines
Zero retraining across multi-type fabric operations enables immediate deployment — factories see ROI from the first month of installation
Retrofit hardware attaches to existing inspection machines with plug-and-play installation — 60% lower CAPEX than traditional systems, zero downtime
Active learning loop plus real-time quality dashboard provides human-aligned judgment and operational transparency for factory floor decision-making
Zero-shot AI achieves 96% AUROC across 16 fabric types (92,982 datapoints) — detects defects from day one without retraining for new fabric types
85% of textile factories rely on manual inspection, missing 40% of defects and leaking 2-5% of revenue to brand chargebacks and compensation claims
A typical factory receives 3,750 rolls monthly but only inspects 20% at $2.50/roll — the unchecked 80% enters production causing $100K+ annual QC losses
Existing AI solutions require per-fabric-type labeled data and full machine replacement — cost and complexity barriers that block SME factory adoption
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: Binh Duong hosts 1,500+ factories in tight cluster; single deployment spreads virally through Korean FDI mill operator community
SEA wage inflation 9%+ YoY eliminates manual inspection economics; EU DPP and brand mandates create quality digitization demand
Zero-shot model is fabric-agnostic; retrofit CAPEX elimination enables SME adoption from Vietnam to Bangladesh without retraining

Every factory in this pool runs the same manual inspection process — zero purpose-built AI inspection solution exists at SME scale
Mid and small factories segmented by device count — each tier priced to match capital capacity
Southeast Asia is the beachhead — 62,550 factories concentrated in the world's fastest-growing production hub
slide_4_Market_2_3_Competition: Fabric inspection product line with 150-year heritage; deep installed base in spinning and weaving quality systems- high precision / high cost
$27.6M raised; automation + traceability + sustainability combined; strongest AI textile benchmark but knit-origin - requires pre-calibration
Fast-deploy on-device industrial vision with GenAI tools; edge processing and low-data messaging overlaps AutoLabs - generic AI inspection
Korean textile AI inspection with smart factory integration; closest local competitor with direct manufacturing AI overlap - custom deployments and mid-market
Zero-shot anomaly detection across 16 fabric types without retraining — competitors require labeled datasets or are locked to single fabric categories
Retrofit hardware installs on existing machines at 60% lower CAPEX with zero downtime — removes primary SME adoption barrier that closed systems create
Edge processing eliminates cloud dependency; roll-level defect logs build quality data layer — inspection evolves into quality data infrastructure over time
slide_5_Market_3_3_GTM_Pricing_BM_Scale: Turnkey HW+SW: $15K-$30K per device (63-75% gross margin)
Blended ACV $48K per customer (3 devices x $1K base + $1K overage x 12 months)
SaaS: $150-300/device/month; usage-based billing at 300K yards/month baseline
HW one-time sale plus monthly SaaS subscription achieves $48K blended ACV per customer with minimal COGS
OEM partnership consolidates supply chain and minimizes sales overhead — one deal unlocks hundreds of factories
Stage 1: Binh Duong OEM partner contract — Songwol Towel Vietnam reference enables horizontal expansion across the 1,500+ factory cluster
Stage 2: Anchor 10 program (10% deposit) accelerates PoC-to-contract velocity; Korea Pulp $86K LOC drives domestic market entry
Stage 3: Korea pulp success plus Binh Duong references unlock multi-factory SEA network expansion to Cambodia, Indonesia, Bangladesh
OEM-enabled hardware externalizes manufacturing costs; zero-shot model eliminates per-customer retraining — near-zero marginal cost per deployment
Multi-fabric data synthesis across factories compounds detection accuracy toward 98%+ each new deployment strengthens the AI moat
Binh Duong to Korea pulp to broader SEA enables density-driven horizontal scaling — reference customers accelerate cluster conversion

slide_9_Team_4_4_Strengths_&_Improvement: ● Retrofit cuts CAPEX 60% vs closed systems
Install on existing machines with zero downtime — directly addresses SME adoption barrier that blocks Uster/Smartex entry
EU DPP, brand mandates, SEA wage inflation 9%+ YoY converge — problem timing validated by Bain, BCG, McKinsey reports
LOC with 90% expected conversion; official site shows $393K cumulative revenue and Daesung TS MOU signed Jan 2026
Official site confirms HW Engineer, FE/BE developers now on team; prior hardware talent gap partially addressed
Anomaly detection on fabric well-supported in literature; ZJU-Leaper dataset and open-set research validate approach
96% AUROC is benchmark metric; real factory false positive rate, speed tolerance, and retuning needs unconfirmed
Uster has fabric inspection products; Smartex raised $27.6M; Shelton Vision, Overview.ai, Stigma all address QC space
Device sales drive early revenue; monthly SaaS $150-300/device is thin — pure SaaS multiples do not apply to model

slide_10_Investor_Assessment: Large, global and structurally recurring need across textile manufacturing with clear cost and quality impact
True scale depends on adoption across fragmented SME factories.
Existing solutions are hardware-heavy or data-dependent, but none dominate the SME segment cleanly
Competitive pressure will increase as incumbents and OEMs integrate AI directly into their machines
Early defensibility comes from zero-shot capability and retrofit advantage enabling faster adoption
Long-term moat depends on data accumulation, OEM integration, and maintaining performance
Strong signals with LOCs, PoCs, and OEM partnerships indicating real demand and willingness to pay.
Still pre-scale with key risks around conversion rates and sustained deployment performance.
Highly scalable due to standardized factory workflows and global similarity of QC processes.
Execution risk lies in hardware deployment, channel partnerships, and maintaining consistency
Balanced team with strong sales hustle and credible technical capability in anomaly detection.
Some risk remains around hardware execution depth and scaling beyond early pilots.

Strong economic value creation with clear ROI, but current pricing undercaptures long-term value, especially on the SaaS layer. Early model is optimized for adoption with hardware-led revenue, creating risk of revenue drop-off post-installation. Upside depends on transitioning to value- or usage-based pricing once performance and trust are established. Inevitabile solution / Team has potential
